w:r.w:t: Nowadays, one of the most important ways to control students’ education and performance is following the
, on time, making assignments, and without limitations and extra difficulties have the possibility
to ask the professor about missing topics and other things.
Knowing these requirements for ideal performance during the semester in Woosong University can lead to chaos in controlling the schedule, which
students have and how to communicate, and when the professor can notice students’ messages.
mobile platform connecting professors and students through a structured syllabus, schedule, and communication system.
All course information lives in one place — accessible on
SyllaSync is a streamlined web platform designed to centralize course schedules, assignments, and communication between professors and students at Woosong University.

Students often miss schedule changes, homework deadlines, and lesson notes because information is scattered across emails, group chats, and paper handouts. Professors lack a structured channel to broadcast updates and track student awareness.
SyllaSync centralises everything into one platform.
Give professors a single dashboard to manage all course communications
Let students view their full academic schedule in a unified calendar
Enable per-course messaging between professors and students
Deliver real-time notifications for schedule changes and new homework
Work seamlessly on both web browsers and mobile devices
Professor dashboard: post schedule changes, homework, lesson notes, announcements
Student dashboard: unified calendar view, homework feed, notes viewer
Per-course chat between professors and enrolled students
Push notifications for changes and new content
The User Interface consists of three main parts:
Navigation Shell, the Content Area, and the Chat Panel. All three are shared across web and mobile, with role-appropriate controls shown per user.
List of all courses taught this semester
Unread chat messages badge per course
Edit course name, description, syllabus PDF
Add / edit / cancel lesson slots with room and time
Post or edit homework: title, description, due date, optional file
Attach rich-text notes to individual lessons
Compose broadcast message to all enrolled students
History of past announcements with timestamps
Threaded real-time message stream per course
Professor name always shown with a distinct label
Supports text messages and image attachments
Tap lesson to see notes, room, and any changes
Chronological list of all assignments across courses
Tap to view full description and download file
Filter by course or due date
Homework list specific to this course
Real-time chat stream for the enrolled course
Can read all messages and send own messages
Push notification on new professor message
Role selector at registration: Student or Professor
Tap to navigate to the relevant screen
Each actor is described with their position in the system, what they interact with, and the actions they can perform. Actions map directly to system functions and API endpoints.
Attaches file to homework (PDF or image, max 10 MB)
Sends course announcement to all enrolled students
Opens and participates in course chat
Opens and navigates the unified calendar
Filters calendar by course or week
Taps a lesson to see notes, room, and schedule changes
Views the homework feed across all courses
Taps homework to read full description and download file
Reads lesson notes attached to lessons
Opens and participates in course chat
Joins a course via professor invite code
Backend services — no direct human interaction.
Sends push notification when professor posts a schedule change
Sends push notification when new homework is published
Broadcasts chat messages via WebSocket to all participants
Validates JWT token on every authenticated request
Stores uploaded files and returns CDN URLs
Returns paginated message history on demand
Key Use Case Flow — Schedule Change
The following describes the end-to-end flow when a professor reschedules a lesson:
Professor opens Course Editor and taps the lesson to reschedule.
Professor updates date, time, and room, then confirms the change.
App sends PUT /lessons/:id to the API with the new values.
Backend updates the database and queues a push notification for all enrolled students.
Students receive the push notification, open the app, and see the updated calendar.
The Student Calendar reflects the change immediately (via polling or lightweight real-time service).
uses a standard client–server architecture with a shared REST API backend serving both web and mobile. Real-time chat is handled via WebSocket (Socket.IO).
Next.js 14/15 (App Router) using Tailwind CSS and
The schema is designed to manage 119+ students across multiple courses efficiently:
Next.js UI for Professor and Student views.
Next.js API Routes, MySQL schema management, and chat logic.
To ensure the successful development of
within the June 15 deadline, the team requests the following support from supervisors, stakeholders, and the university.
Request 1 — Access to Real Course Data for Testing

Access to sample course schedules, lesson structures, and homework formats from actual university courses will allow the team to validate the calendar and homework feed with realistic data, improving accuracy before the final demo.
Request 2 — Regular Feedback from Supervising Professor

Biweekly check-ins, or async feedback on progress reports, from the supervising professor will ensure the system's features align with actual teaching workflows and institutional expectations. This prevents late-stage redesigns.

Clear documentation of what the final submission should include — live demo, code repository, written report, or presentation deck — will help the team allocate the final two weeks appropriately without last-minute surprises.

Access to 5–10 student volunteers willing to test the application during Week 9 user acceptance testing will provide realistic usage patterns and surface usability issues that the development team cannot identify internally.
Request 5 — Guidance on Vibe Coding Boundaries

The team uses AI-assisted (vibe) coding for boilerplate and scaffolding. Clarity on how much AI-generated code is acceptable in the final submission, and whether it should be disclosed, will help the team code responsibly and avoid academic integrity concerns.
Request 6 — Free-Tier Hosting Access or University Server

Hosting the backend and database on Railway or Render free tier may introduce cold-start latency during the demo. If the university can provide a small server or cloud credits, a more stable and reliable demo experience is possible.
are the trickiest piece; start Socket.IO setup
Mobile build issues — updates help, but test on real devices early, not just in week 9
API contract drift — frontend and backend changes must be coordinated;
Calendar dashboard updating in real time – calendar must be updated according to new updates and show only real and approved data
